proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_078 (range 30-90, mean 65, plausible age) is positively associated with pfs_months across the cohort.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import spearmanr; spearmanr(df['feature_078'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.853 between feature_078 and pfs_months; mean PFS rises monotonically across age tertiles (low=1.84, mid=3.72, high=5.69 months). Massive positive association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The continuous variable feature_013 (heavily right-skewed, range 0.1-3622, plausible PSA) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
The continuous variable feature_009 (range 1.5-5.5, plausible serum albumin) is positively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: spearmanr(np.log1p(df['feature_013']), df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.223 between feature_013 and pfs_months; PFS falls monotonically across feature_013 quartiles (Q1=4.31, Q2=3.93, Q3=3.62, Q4=3.11 months). Strongly negative.

analyses.code: spearmanr(df['feature_009'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = +0.118 between feature_009 and pfs_months. Significant positive association consistent with a protective biomarker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006 (range 0-24.6, mean 3.84, right-skewed) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.code: spearmanr(df['feature_006'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Spearman rho = -0.134 between feature_006 and pfs_months. Modestly negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 3-level ordinal feature_057 (values 0/1/2, plausible disease stage or risk group) is negatively associated with pfs_months: each increment lowers PFS.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_057')['pfs_months'].mean(); spearmanr(df['feature_057'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_057 level: 0=4.68 (n=17592), 1=3.49 (n=24971), 2=2.38 (n=7437). Spearman rho = -0.386. Each level decrement of ~1.2 months PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature_051 (prevalence 55%) has a strong negative univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 4.03 (feature_051=0) vs 3.51 (feature_051=1); difference -0.52 months. Welch t-test p ~ 1.6e-181. Strongest single binary main effect by p-value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The binary feature_109 (prevalence 10%) has a positive univariate association with pfs_months consistent with a beneficial intervention.
The binary feature_039 (prevalence 10%) has a positive univariate association with pfs_months.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_109']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_109']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 3.73 (feature_109=0) vs 3.83 (feature_109=1); univariate diff = +0.100 months, p = 2.05e-3.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(df.loc[df['feature_039']==1,'pfs_months'], df.loc[df['feature_039']==0,'pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS: 3.73 (feature_039=0) vs 3.83 (feature_039=1); univariate diff = +0.094 months, p = 3.29e-3.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_018 (5 categories: white/black/hispanic/asian/other), suggesting a race-like demographic effect.
Mean pfs_months differs across categories of feature_045 (medicare/private/medicaid/uninsured), suggesting an insurance-related effect.

analyses.code: f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_018']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_018'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_018: white=3.741, black=3.741, hispanic=3.737, asian=3.797, other=3.749. One-way ANOVA F=0.564, p=0.689. No detectable main effect of race-like grouping on PFS.

analyses.code: f_oneway(*[df.loc[df['feature_045']==v,'pfs_months'] for v in df['feature_045'].unique()])
analyses.result_summary: Mean PFS by feature_045: medicare=3.754, medicaid=3.741, private=3.736, uninsured=3.726. One-way ANOVA F=0.344, p=0.794. No detectable main effect of insurance-like grouping on PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS model containing feature_078 (age), log feature_013 (PSA-like), log feature_006, feature_009, and feature_057 (stage) explains the bulk of variance in pfs_months, and each predictor remains independently significant.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: OLS R^2 = 0.966 with 5 predictors. Coefficients: feature_078 +0.174 (p<1e-300); log_f013 -0.382 (p<1e-300); log_f006 -0.302 (p<1e-300); feature_009 +0.478 (p<1e-300); feature_057 -1.152 (p<1e-300). Each predictor large and independently significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for feature_078, log feature_013, log feature_006, feature_009, and feature_057, the negative effect of feature_051 on pfs_months persists.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 coefficient drops from -0.521 (univariate) to -0.0005 (adjusted), p = 0.895. Adjusted effect is essentially zero — the apparent univariate association is fully explained by the other covariates.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The disappearance of feature_051's effect after adjustment is driven specifically by confounding with feature_013 (PSA-like): patients with feature_051=1 have substantially higher feature_013.

analyses.code: ttest_ind(np.log1p(df.loc[df['feature_051']==0,'feature_013']), np.log1p(df.loc[df['feature_051']==1,'feature_013']))
analyses.result_summary: Mean log(feature_013): 2.175 in feature_051=0 vs 3.552 in feature_051=1, p ~ 0 (essentially infinite). Sequentially adding covariates shows feature_051 coefficient: alone = -0.521, +feature_078 = -0.518, +log_f013 = +0.011 (p=0.30, no longer significant). feature_013 alone explains the apparent feature_051 effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_109 has an INDEPENDENT positive effect on pfs_months after adjusting for feature_078, log feature_013, log feature_006, feature_009, and feature_057.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 adjusted coefficient = +0.1526, p = 8.7e-169. Adjusted effect is LARGER than the univariate (+0.100), consistent with negative confounding. Robust positive predictor of PFS.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039 has an INDEPENDENT positive effect on pfs_months after adjusting for the same core covariates.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_039 adjusted coefficient = +0.1470, p = 1.4e-159. Adjusted effect is +0.147 vs univariate +0.094, again amplified by adjustment. Robust positive predictor.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_043 (prevalence 20%, small univariate negative association) retains a small negative effect on pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_043 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_043 adjusted coefficient = -0.0166, p = 6.1e-5. Effect is tiny (less than 1/60th of a feature_057 step) but statistically robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for the core covariates, the multi-category feature_018 (race-like) and feature_045 (insurance-like) variables remain non-predictive of pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_018) + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients of feature_018 levels (vs 'asian' reference): black +0.0001 (p=0.99), hispanic +0.0002 (p=0.98), other +0.0055 (p=0.69), white +0.0015 (p=0.83). Joint contribution near zero. Same null result for feature_045 (medicare -0.0005 p=0.92; private -0.0002 p=0.97; uninsured +0.0004 p=0.96). No demographic or insurance disparity in PFS detectable in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (now null) effect of feature_051 on pfs_months differs across feature_018 (race-like) groups, i.e., heterogeneous treatment effect by race.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051*C(feature_018) + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficients: feature_051:black +0.023 p=0.17, feature_051:hispanic -0.002 p=0.89, feature_051:other -0.007 p=0.81, feature_051:white +0.010 p=0.49. None significant. No evidence of differential effect of feature_051 by race.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_109 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across feature_018 (race-like) groups.
The positive effect of feature_039 on pfs_months is heterogeneous across feature_018 (race-like) groups.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*C(feature_018) + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 main effect (in 'asian' reference) = +0.145, p=2.6e-11. Interaction coefficients: black -0.009 p=0.74, hispanic -0.006 p=0.81, other +0.029 p=0.52, white +0.014 p=0.53. All non-significant. Effect of feature_109 appears uniform across racial groups.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_039*C(feature_018) + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_039 main effect = +0.157, p=6.9e-13. Interaction coefficients: black -0.032 p=0.24, hispanic -0.016 p=0.54, other +0.049 p=0.30, white -0.007 p=0.78. All non-significant. Effect of feature_039 appears uniform across racial groups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs by feature_057 (stage), with greater benefit at higher stage.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*feature_057 + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 main effect = +0.147 (p~10^-66), feature_109:feature_057 interaction = +0.0073 (p=0.36). No significant heterogeneity by stage.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs by age (feature_078).

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*feature_078 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 main effect = +0.128 at age 0 reference (p=3.5e-4); feature_109:feature_078 = +0.0004, p = 0.50. No age × feature_109 interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_109 on pfs_months differs by baseline log feature_013 (PSA-like): patients with higher feature_013 derive less benefit.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*np.log1p(feature_013) + feature_078 + log_f006 + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 main effect = +0.179 (p~10^-37); feature_109:log_f013 interaction = -0.0089, p = 0.043. Marginal evidence of mild attenuation of benefit at higher feature_013, but the interaction magnitude is small relative to the main effect (~5% reduction per unit of log feature_013).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The (null) effect of feature_051 on pfs_months interacts with feature_057 (stage), implying that any residual signal varies by disease severity.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_051*feature_057 + feature_078 + log_f013 + log_f006 + feature_009', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 main effect = -0.022 (p=8e-5); feature_051:feature_057 = +0.027 (p=4e-8). Statistically significant but very small interaction; the signed pattern indicates that the (already small) negative association weakens at higher stage. Practical magnitude is on the order of 0.03 months PFS per stage step — clinically negligible despite the low p-value driven by n=50000.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Other binary features that showed marginal univariate signals (feature_105, feature_115, feature_104, feature_032, feature_023) retain independent effects on pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: for c in ['feature_105','feature_115','feature_104','feature_032','feature_023']: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ '+c+' + feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_043 + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficients/p-values: feature_105 -0.003 p=0.69; feature_115 +0.002 p=0.68; feature_104 +0.007 p=0.11; feature_032 +0.002 p=0.68; feature_023 +0.006 p=0.24. None retains significance; all marginal univariate signals dissolve under adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the multi-valued ordinal features (feature_067, feature_016, feature_071, feature_026, feature_096, feature_033, feature_064), at least one besides feature_057 has an independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: for c in multi: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ '+c+' + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted slope per unit step: feature_067 -0.0104 (p=1.7e-10, weak negative); feature_016 +0.0013 p=0.38; feature_071 +0.0018 p=0.23; feature_026 +0.0004 p=0.50; feature_096 -0.0014 p=0.35; feature_033 +0.0011 p=0.46; feature_064 +0.0009 p=0.58. Only feature_067 shows a small but robust negative association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among continuous lab-like features (feature_038, feature_117, feature_103, feature_120, feature_054, feature_062, feature_031, feature_082, feature_118, feature_005, feature_122), one or more have independent effects on pfs_months after adjustment.

analyses.code: for c in labs: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ '+c+' + ... covariates', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted slopes: feature_038 -2e-4 p=0.79; feature_117 +2e-4 p=0.66; feature_103 +0.004 p=0.24; feature_120 -3e-5 p=0.25; feature_054 +8e-5 p=0.49; feature_062 +2e-4 p=0.30; feature_031 -3e-4 p=0.018 (marginal); feature_082 +1.4e-3 p=0.085; feature_118 -1e-5 p=0.98; feature_005 -6e-4 p=0.30; feature_122 +3e-5 p=0.66. No lab provides meaningful incremental information over the core 5-predictor model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A final adjusted model containing feature_078, log feature_013, log feature_006, feature_009, feature_057, feature_109, feature_039, feature_043, and feature_067 explains essentially all systematic variance in pfs_months and contains all independently significant predictors identified in the dataset.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109 + feature_039 + feature_043 + feature_067 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Final OLS R^2 = 0.96749 (n=50000). Coefficients (all p<<0.001 except where noted): feature_078 +0.1738; feature_057 -1.1523; log_f013 -0.3825; log_f006 -0.3032; feature_009 +0.4772; feature_109 +0.1519; feature_039 +0.1464; feature_043 -0.0167; feature_067 -0.0104. Adding any further binary, ordinal, lab, race, or insurance variable yields negligible incremental R^2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The strongest two beneficial 'treatment-like' binary features (feature_109 and feature_039) act additively rather than redundantly on pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_109*feature_039 + feature_078 + np.log1p(feature_013) + np.log1p(feature_006) + feature_009 + feature_057', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: feature_109 +0.152, feature_039 +0.146, feature_109:feature_039 interaction effectively zero (the joint additive model already gives R^2 = 0.96746 with both at full magnitude and feature_043, feature_067 included; adding the interaction term changes coefficients negligibly and does not improve fit). Conclusion: the two beneficial features act additively, not synergistically.
